evals.prompt: Estou sempre ocupado mas sem avançar. Quero um Perfect Week que me proteja o tempo de trabalho profundo.
evals.expected_output: A calendar audit against the five defense rules, a Perfect Week template following all five rules, a Preloaded Year skeleton for the next quarter, and a recurring Friday 30-min review block set up.
expectations: The skill calls kb_search for the company profile, any prior Perfect Week template, and the Dan Martell/Alex Hormozi/Sabri Suby frameworks before auditing.
The skill requests the founder's current week calendar (screenshot, export, or verbal description) before auditing — it does not audit a hypothetical week.
The audit identifies every block that violates any of the five defense rules by name (Rule 1 through Rule 5).
The Perfect Week template is structured with Monday/Friday as shallow days and Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday as deep days, with the First-Four-Hours block marked [PROTECTED].
The Preloaded Year skeleton blocks at minimum weekly, monthly, quarterly, and annual recurring commitments for the next quarter.

evals.prompt: Audit my calendar for next week: Monday I have a team sync at 10am, Tuesday I have a client call at 10am, Wednesday is free, Thursday has a proposal review at 3pm, Friday I have admin the whole day.
evals.expected_output: A violation report against all five rules for the described week, with specific blocks named per violation and a corrected Perfect Week layout.
expectations: The Tuesday client call at 10am is identified as violating Rule 2 (First-Four-Hours sacred) — a meeting lands in the deep-work block on a deep-work day.
Friday 'admin the whole day' is evaluated against Rule 3 (Friday is a shallow day — admin is correct for Friday, so this may pass Rule 3).
The Monday team sync at 10am is evaluated against Rule 2 — if it is within the first four hours, it is flagged as a violation.
Every violation is named with the specific rule number and the specific block that breaks it.
The corrected Perfect Week pushes violating meetings to Mon/Fri afternoon slots or after the First-Four-Hours block.

evals.prompt: Devo aceitar esta reunião de cliente na terça-feira de manhã às 9h? Dura 1 hora.
evals.expected_output: A calendar-defense assessment of the proposed meeting slot against the five rules, with a clear accept/decline recommendation and an alternative slot if declined.
expectations: The skill checks the meeting slot against Rule 2 (First-Four-Hours sacred — 9h on Tuesday is in the protected block) and explicitly identifies the violation.
The skill checks Rule 3 (Tuesday is a deep-work day — a client meeting is shallow work and should be on Mon/Fri).
The recommendation is either accept with explicit justification of the exception cost, or decline with a concrete alternative slot (e.g. Monday 14h or Friday 14h).
The 80% cap check (Rule 5) is mentioned if the founder's calendar context is known.
The skill does not accept 'the client insists' as sufficient justification without naming the cost of the exception.
